title: Jacob Elordi on His Frankenstein Transformation
text: Jacob Elordi underwent an extensive physical metamorphosis to portray the Creature in Guillermo del Toro's Frankenstein, which earned him a 2026 Golden Globe nomination for Best Supporting Actor. The 28-year-old actor spent considerable daily time in makeup using 42 prosthetics to achieve the character's grotesque appearance. Elordi described the experience: Your skin gets washed out and covered with something new. Your face, and its structure, change. He emphasized that donning the costume featuring leather, furs, and considerable weight was a complete sensory undertaking that began the moment he entered the makeup trailer. Rather than viewing the extensive prosthetics as merely technical, Elordi framed the preparation as deeply meaningful. Director del Toro instructed him to approach the process ceremonially, comparing it to priestly robing rituals. This perspective transformed the daily makeup application into what he called a metamorphosis and a meditation. The demanding role reignited Elordi's passion for filmmaking. He reflected: I'll remember this forever. It completely reignited my passion for movies.
